Pure Storage Executive Brief - Pure Launch
A rundown of the Helio testing activities over the last week. 
ZURB  |  August 14th, 2024

Advocacy Process
Test & Learn
Advocates use hunches to 
formulate questions for 
participants. Design concepts

Observations are synthesized 
from their answers. 
Signals shape the direction of 
the products and services.
Testing is done in Helio

pages.text:
All information copyright Pure Storage and ZURB, inc.
Pure Storage Executive Brief
A rundown of the Helio testing activities over the last month. 
ZURB  |  March 5th, 2026

Does the new homepage & platform 
page design outperform the old 
versions?
UX Metrics
● Comprehension
● Sentiment  
Audiences 
● Technical leader (US, UK, GR & FR)
● AI Infrastructure and Technical Leaders (Global)
● Infrastructure Owner (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Front-end & Full Stack Developers (Global)
● Non-Technical Leaders (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Financial Decision Makers (US)
Rebrand: Homepage
Concept 176

The homepage and platform pages 
outperform the old designs.
Assumption
● New color scheme feels less abrasive than past 
orange-heavy designs
● Better spacing lets the page breath.
Rebrand Redesigns 
Hunch Concept 176

Experiment with new hero copy on the 
homepage:
Low impressions of conversational and friendly 
bring down the overall sentiment, mostly due to 
the messaging on the page.
Rebrand Redesigns
Recommendation
 Concept 176
Source: Data Comparison Framework

The homepage redesign is 
polarizing for visitors who 
understand the oﬀer but don’t 
feel the messaging is friendly 
or conversational. 
Sentiment of the newly designed 
homepage dropped from about 50% 
to 33%, despite similar 
comprehension scores as the 
previous version.
Rebrand Redesigns
Signal
 Concept 176
Source: Data Comparison Framework

Removing the pop-ups and 
banners from the new 
homepage increases positive 
sentiment. 
Sentiment increased on the current 
version of the homepage from 33% 
to 43% when removing some of the 
busy components at the top of the 
page.
Rebrand Redesigns
Signal
 Concept 176
Source: Data Comparison Framework

Rebrand Redesigns
Decisions
Implement
Reﬁne Design
Test Iteration
Revisit Later
Do Not Pursue
Concept 176
Test Iteration
Change hero copy.
-> Easy to test, diﬃcult to implement
Source: Data Comparison Framework

Does the new homepage & platform 
page design outperform the old 
versions?
UX Metrics
● Comprehension
● Sentiment  
Audiences 
● Technical leader (US, UK, GR & FR)
● AI Infrastructure and Technical Leaders (Global)
● Infrastructure Owner (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Front-end & Full Stack Developers (Global)
● Non-Technical Leaders (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Financial Decision Makers (US)
Rebrand: Platform Page
Concept 176

The homepage and platform pages 
outperform the old designs.
Assumption
● New color scheme feels less abrasive than past 
orange-heavy designs
● Better spacing lets the page breath.
● The infographic may be too overwhelming
Rebrand Redesigns 
Hunch Concept 176

Iterate on hero copy to clarify product 
oﬀering:
Low sentiment stems from the lack of clarity in 
messaging about the product.
Simplify infographic to present no more 
than 12 items for users to understand:
From our previous infographic testing, we know 
that graphics with more layers to understand test 
poorly. The much simpler graphic on the 
rebranded EDC page received very positive 
reactions.
Rebrand Redesigns
Recommendation
 Concept 176
Source: Data Comparison Framework

Low sentiment towards the 
new platform page stems 
from the content feeling 
overly technical and too much 
content competing for 
attention. 
Sentiment of the newly designed 
platform page dropped from about 
53% to 32%, despite similar 
comprehension scores as the 
previous version. Users explained 
that they don’t know where they 
should direct their attention ﬁrst, 
they feel the language is too 
technical/advanced, and there are 
too many diﬀerent content modules.
Rebrand Redesigns
Signal
 Concept 176
Source: Data Comparison Framework
“the language used can be 
slightly complicated to digest if 
you don't know about large data 
management. Terms I was 
unfamiliar with were things like 
"copy creep" and 
"mission-critical workloads"”
“site appears capable and 
advanced, oﬀering strong 
enterprises solutions, but the 
technical language and features 
makes it somewhat complex to 
understand.”
“Plenty of information but very 
chaotic.”
“Some of these content blocks 
could just be referenced instead 
of full builds… Not everything can 
take the spotlight and this 
company needs to recognize 
when to make the real estate 
trade-oﬀs.”

Engagement with the 
infographic is low on ﬁrst click 
and when looking for a 
speciﬁc layer information. 
Only 2% of participants used their 
ﬁrst click on the infographic, and 
10% of participants were able to ﬁnd 
a piece of information in the layers 
of the graphic.
Rebrand Redesigns
Signal
 Concept 176
Source: Data Comparison Framework

Comprehension of the 
platform page infographic is 
reduced by impressions that it 
is for advanced technical 
leads to understand. 
The infographics comprehension 
score was barely out of the average 
range at 70%, with up to 40% of 
participants indicating they 
understood little to none of what it 
means.
Rebrand Redesigns
Signal
 Concept 176
Source: Helio Survey

Users have trouble seeing 
how the information connects 
in the infographic after 
selecting a layer. 
Sentiment for the infographic is 
poor, with spikes above 10% in 
negative impressions of 
complicated, confusing, and 
overwhelming. 
Rebrand Redesigns
Signal
 Concept 176
Source: Helio Survey

Rebrand Redesigns
Decisions
Implement
Reﬁne Design
Test Iteration
Revisit Later
Do Not Pursue
Concept 176
Test Iteration
Reduce items user needs to understand on the infographic (below 13 items)
Reduce large content modules.
Test iterations of copy on page.
Source: Data Comparison Framework

Does the new Evergreen page design 
outperform the old versions?
UX Metrics
● Comprehension
● Appeal
● Sentiment  
Audiences 
● Technical leader (US, UK, GR & FR)
● AI Infrastructure and Technical Leaders (Global)
● Infrastructure Owner (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Front-end & Full Stack Developers (Global)
● Non-Technical Leaders (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Financial Decision Makers (US)
Evergreen Redesign
Concept 178

The Evergreen page may beneﬁt from a 
dark mode approach.
Assumption
● The current redesign mixes dark & light color 
schemes
● The infographic at the top of the current page may 
be confusing to users.
Evergreen Redesign 
Hunch Concept 178

Move forward with new dark mode 
design:
The dark mode signiﬁcantly increased all UX 
metrics tested on the Evergreen page.
Move ROI CTA down page:
Users need more clarity on what is being oﬀered 
before they want to learn about the ROI that it can 
produce.
Evergreen Redesign
Recommendation
 Concept 178
Source: Data Comparison Framework

The dark mode of the 
Evergreen page increases 
appeal towards the page 
while decreasing negative 
impressions of being stiﬀ and 
uncertain. 
UX metrics across the board 
increased for the new variation of 
the Evergreen page, especially in 
terms of sentiment and appeal.
Evergreen Redesign
Signal
 Concept 178
Source: Data Comparison Framework

Visitors aren't interested in 
ROI promises before they 
understand the platform 
oﬀering. 
See Y our ROI Impact was rated one 
of the least helpful pieces of the 
new Evergreen page despite it's 
prominent placement in the hero. 
The 'discover' CTA in the current 
hero is a more interesting CTA for 
visitors.
Evergreen Redesign
Signal
 Concept 178
Source: Data Comparison Framework

SLAs content on the current 
Evergreen page is one of the 
most helpful pieces of 
content. 
Behind the video and how it works 
content, this SLAs section was voted 
as most helpful by a signiﬁcant 
amount of participants reacting to 
the current iteration of the page.
Evergreen Redesign
Signal
 Concept 178
Source: Data Comparison Framework

Increasing infographic 
content at the top of the page 
reduces comprehension and 
sentiment. 
Both versions 2 and 3 of the dark 
mode saw a decrease in 
comprehension and a signiﬁcant 
drop in positive impressions 
towards the page.
Evergreen Redesign
Signal
 Concept 178
Source: Data Comparison Framework

Providing a color-break after 
the hero increases appeal and 
sentiment of the Evergreen 
page. 
We saw the highest sentiment score 
and lowest amount of negative 
impressions when changing the 
second modules background color 
from black to gray.
Evergreen Redesign
Signal
 Concept 178
Source: Data Comparison Framework

Evergreen Redesign
Decisions
Implement
Reﬁne Design
Test Iteration
Revisit Later
Do Not Pursue
Concept 178
Implement
Move forward with Dark Mode V4
Source: Data Comparison Framework
Test Iteration
Apply principles to light mode. SLA’s further down the page.

Does the new EDC page design 
outperform the old versions?
UX Metrics
● Comprehension
● Sentiment  
● Desirability
Audiences 
● Technical leader (US, UK, GR & FR)
● AI Infrastructure and Technical Leaders (Global)
● Infrastructure Owner (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Front-end & Full Stack Developers (Global)
● Non-Technical Leaders (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Financial Decision Makers (US)
EDC Redesign
Concept 179

The new EDC page should outperform the 
old design.
Assumption
● Old hero content doesn’t provide interesting or 
educational material
● New infographic is more clear and easier to parse 
than the old infographic.
EDC Redesign 
Hunch Concept 179

Maintain new redesigned page:
New page slightly outperformed old design across 
each UX metric.
Move EDC infographic further up the 
page:
This is one of the most helpful pieces of content on 
both the old and new versions of the page.
EDC Redesign
Recommendation
 Concept 179
Source: Data Comparison Framework

The new EDC page improves 
comprehension and 
sentiment over the old 
version before the redesign. 
Both versions of the page tested 
well, though the new redesign 
version produced slight increases in 
UX metrics across the board.
EDC Redesign
Signal
 Concept 179

The EDC infographic is one of 
the most clear and helpful 
pieces of content on the page. 
In both the old and newly redesign 
version of the EDC page, the 
infographic was rated as one of, if 
not the most, helpful information on 
the page. Both variations maintain 
this user interest despite their lower 
placement on the page.
EDC Redesign
Signal
 Concept 179

EDC Redesign
Decisions
Implement
Reﬁne Design
Test Iteration
Revisit Later
Do Not Pursue
Concept 179
Test Iteration
Try moving the EDC infographic further up the page to increase comprehension and sentiment

Can visitors ﬁnd the pathﬁnder ﬂow on 
the homepage? Does the experience 
drive intent to engage?
UX Metrics
● Success
● Expectations
● Intent
Audiences 
● Technical leader (US, UK, GR & FR)
● AI Infrastructure and Technical Leaders (Global)
● Infrastructure Owner (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Front-end & Full Stack Developers (Global)
● Non-Technical Leaders (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Financial Decision Makers (US)
Homepage Pathﬁnder Flow
Concept 180

The pathﬁnder is a desirable feature on 
the homepage.
Assumption
● Current placement could be easily missed on the 
homepage
● Users think less of personalizing for themselves, 
and more about getting a custom quote for their 
business
● Ending the ﬂow with a primary CTA to open a chat 
bot is not engaging
Homepage Pathﬁnder Flow 
Hunch Concept 180

Homepage Pathﬁnder Flow
Design Concept 180
Baseline Flow

Provide options outside of a chatbot at 
the end of the pathﬁnder ﬂow:
Intent to engage with the Start a Chat option are 
low, with most participants indicating they want a 
demo from a live rep after completing the 
personalization questions.
Emphasize personalization module on 
homepage:
Only 16% of participants clicked in this module 
compared to the 47% of participants who 
interacted with the top nav CTA.
Homepage Pathﬁnder Flow
Recommendation
 Concept 180
Source: Helio Survey

The option to select cards in 
the homepage Personalize 
module is unclear when 
looking for custom oﬀers. 
An average amount of participants 
were able to successfully ﬁnd the 
Personalize ﬂow from the 
homepage, though only 16% of the 
total 63% engaged with the module 
in the middle of the homepage. Of 
those 16%, only 2 participants 
interacted with the cards in the 
modal, with others instead 
gravitating towards the personalize 
CTA in that section.
Homepage Pathﬁnder Flow
Signal
 Concept 180
Source: Helio Survey

The pathﬁnder ﬂow matches 
well with users' expectations 
for being asked questions 
that will help map to their 
business's needs. 
Expectations score was good at 
79%, with a majority of participants 
indicating they expect some type of 
form or questionnaire that will ask 
them questions about their business 
and data management needs.
Homepage Pathﬁnder Flow
Signal
 Concept 180
Source: Helio Survey
Pathﬁnder

Start a Chat is not the most 
desirable action to take after 
users complete the pathﬁnder 
personalization ﬂow. 
About 20% of participants showed 
intent to start a chat, which was the 
third most desired action from the 
pathﬁnder ﬂow after requesting a 
demo or editing business 
preferences. Many users who would 
want to start a chat expect to get 
support from a live company rep 
rather than a chat bot.
Homepage Pathﬁnder Flow
Signal
 Concept 180
Source: Helio Survey
Next steps

Homepage Pathﬁnder Flow
Decisions
Implement
Reﬁne Design
Test Iteration
Revisit Later
Do Not Pursue
Concept 180
Reﬁne Design
Provide ‘request demo’ or ‘talk to rep’ options after pathﬁnder ﬂow.
Source: Helio Survey
Test Iteration
Try out ‘custom quote’ or ‘customize’ language on CTA to increase engagement.
Make the option to ‘select a use case’ more clear on the homepage module.
Test “Share with Team” feature to socialize the resources.

Will users have a strong opinion about 
which URL to use for the Knowledge 
Portal domain?
UX Metrics
● Expectations
● Sentiment
Audiences 
● Technical leader (US, UK, GR & FR)
● AI Infrastructure and Technical Leaders (Global)
● Infrastructure Owner (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Front-end & Full Stack Developers (Global)
● Non-Technical Leaders (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Financial Decision Makers (US)
Knowledge Portal Domain
Concept 181
 URL: https://support.everpuredata.com

Users may have a strong opinion about 
which URL is used for the Knowledge 
Portal.
Assumption
● The URL matters
● The URL aﬀects how users search for this 
information
Homepage Pathﬁnder Flow 
Hunch Concept 181
https://knowledge.everpuredata.com
https://support.everpuredata.com

Maintain ‘support’ URL for Knowledge 
Portal:
Expectations were even for both versions of the 
URL, though sentiment was 10% higher for the 
‘support’ version compared to the ‘knowledge’ 
version.
Homepage Pathﬁnder Flow
Recommendation
 Concept 181
Source: Support Test & Knowledge Test
https://support.everpuredata.com

Users are comfortable with 
the URL choice regardless of 
the expectations they 
establish for how they would 
search. 
Both versions of the URL did well to 
match participants expectations with 
a low 70% score for each.
Homepage Pathﬁnder Flow
Signal
 Concept 181
‘Support’ URL‘Knowledge’ URL
Source: Support Test & Knowledge Test

Knowledge in the URL 
provides less clarity for users 
searching for Everpure 
support information. 
This variation of the support home 
URL produced a spike in negative 
impressions of being confusing and 
a signiﬁcant drop in positive 
impressions of clarity.

Signal
 Concept 181
‘Support’ URL‘Knowledge’ URL
Source: Knowledge Test & Support Test

Homepage Pathﬁnder Flow
Decisions
Implement
Reﬁne Design
Test Iteration
Revisit Later
Do Not Pursue
Concept 181
Do Not Pursue
Quelyn to work with IT team to shift knowledge portal from https://support.purestorage.com/ to 
https://support.everpuredata.com. We saw a positive 15% shift in Clarity and a 4% shift in 
Confusion when participants expressed their sentiment about the Support domain when 
compared against the knowledge.everpuredata.com domain.

Do users have a strong preference 
between one-page scroll or single topic 
views in the Knowledge Portal?
UX Metrics
● Preference
Audiences 
● Technical leader (US, UK, GR & FR)
● AI Infrastructure and Technical Leaders (Global)
● Infrastructure Owner (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Front-end & Full Stack Developers (Global)
● Non-Technical Leaders (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Financial Decision Makers (US)
Inﬁnite Scroll
Concept 183

Users have a preference for one scroll 
options versus another.
Assumption
● There may be an even split for single topic vs 
one-page scroll
● Single topic keeps users more focused
● One-page scroll eliminates extra clicks
Inﬁnite Scroll
Hunch Concept 183

Move forward with single topic view as 
default:
This was the view preferred by the majority of 
participants, though some said they would still 
want the choice between scrolling options, and 
others indicated the diﬀerence doesn’t matter to 
them.
Inﬁnite Scroll
Recommendation
 Concept 183
Source: Inﬁnite Scroll Test

The single topic view allows 
users to concentrate on 
speciﬁc topics without getting 
lost in the content. 
Up to 44% of participants indicated 
they would prefer the single topic 
view compared to just 28% of votes 
for the one-page scroll. Participants 
explained that this will make it 
easier for them to focus on the 
individual topics.
Inﬁnite Scroll
Signal
 Concept 183
Source: Inﬁnite Scroll Test
“I prefer the single topic view. it allows me to 
concentrate on one piece of information at a time 
and thoroughly assimilate the content. It's also 
easier to revisit the information again”
“I prefer the single-topic view because you know 
what the beginning and end are. There is not a lot 
of material that does not pertain to your article; I 
ﬁnd it easier to read”
“Single topic view. It makes navigating the page 
seemless”
“I prefer the single topic view cause I like to ﬁnish 
one thing ﬁrst before moving to the next so it 
helped me focus”

Some users are worried about 
losing their place using 
one-page scroll. 
Participants who indicated a 
preference for single topic view said 
that one-page scroll might let them 
get lost when trying to absorb 
information across multiple topics.
Inﬁnite Scroll
Signal
 Concept 183
Source: Inﬁnite Scroll Test
“Single topic view because I don't want for the 
scroll to go too fast or to get away from me. 
Sometimes I need to go back and read something 
twice”
“Single topic view because it allows you to ﬁnd 
exactly what you need instantly without the 
frustration of hunting through a massive Wall of 
text”
“Single topic scroll seems to be more eﬃcient with 
less scrolling needed.”
“Single topic because it's easy to ﬁnd my place 
again”

Inﬁnite Scroll
Decisions
Implement
Reﬁne Design
Test Iteration
Revisit Later
Do Not Pursue
Concept 183
Reﬁne Design
Quelyn and team to implement single-topic view as the default scrolling mode in the Knowledge 
Portal. The single-topic view was the preference for a majority of participants, based on the 
security of consuming important content without losing their place from long scrolls.
Source: Inﬁnite Scroll Test

Does the new homepage & platform 
page design outperform the old 
versions?
UX Metrics

Concept 176


The homepage and platform pages 
outperform the old designs.
Assumption

orange-heavy designs
● Better spacing lets the page breath.

Hunch Concept 176


Experiment with new hero copy on the 
homepage:
Low impressions of conversational and friendly 
bring down the overall sentiment, mostly due to 
the messaging on the page.

Source: Data Comparison Framework


The homepage redesign is 
polarizing for visitors who 
understand the oﬀer but don’t 
feel the messaging is friendly 
or conversational. 
Sentiment of the newly designed 
homepage dropped from about 50% 
to 33%, despite similar 
comprehension scores as the 
previous version.
Rebrand Redesigns
Signal
 Concept 176
Source: Data Comparison Framework


Removing the pop-ups and 
banners from the new 
homepage increases positive 
sentiment. 
Sentiment increased on the current 
version of the homepage from 33% 
to 43% when removing some of the 
busy components at the top of the 
page.

Signal
 Concept 176

Implement

Test Iteration

Does the new homepage & platform 
page design outperform the old 
versions?
UX Metrics
● Comprehension
● Sentiment  
Audiences 
● Technical leader (US, UK, GR & FR)

Concept 176


The homepage and platform pages 
outperform the old designs.
Assumption
● New color scheme feels less abrasive than past 
orange-heavy designs
● Better spacing lets the page breath.
● The infographic may be too overwhelming
Rebrand Redesigns 
Hunch Concept 176


Iterate on hero copy to clarify product 
oﬀering:
Low sentiment stems from the lack of clarity in 
messaging about the product.
Simplify infographic to present no more 
than 12 items for users to understand:
From our previous infographic testing, we know 
that graphics with more layers to understand test 
poorly. The much simpler graphic on the 
rebranded EDC page received very positive 
reactions.
Rebrand Redesigns
Recommendation
 Concept 176
Source: Data Comparison Framework

Low sentiment towards the 
new platform page stems 
from the content feeling 
overly technical and too much 
content competing for 
attention. 
Sentiment of the newly designed 
platform page dropped from about 
53% to 32%, despite similar 
comprehension scores as the 
previous version. Users explained 
that they don’t know where they 
should direct their attention ﬁrst, 
they feel the language is too 
technical/advanced, and there are 
too many diﬀerent content modules.
Rebrand Redesigns
Signal
 Concept 176
Source: Data Comparison Framework
“the language used can be 
slightly complicated to digest if 
you don't know about large data 
management. Terms I was 
unfamiliar with were things like 
"copy creep" and 
"mission-critical workloads"”
“site appears capable and 
advanced, oﬀering strong 
enterprises solutions, but the 
technical language and features 
makes it somewhat complex to 
understand.”
“Plenty of information but very 
chaotic.”
“Some of these content blocks 
could just be referenced instead 
of full builds… Not everything can 
take the spotlight and this 
company needs to recognize 
when to make the real estate 
trade-oﬀs.”

Engagement with the 
infographic is low on ﬁrst click 
and when looking for a 
speciﬁc layer information. 
Only 2% of participants used their 
ﬁrst click on the infographic, and 
10% of participants were able to ﬁnd 
a piece of information in the layers 
of the graphic.
Rebrand Redesigns
Signal
 Concept 176
Source: Data Comparison Framework


Comprehension of the 
platform page infographic is 
reduced by impressions that it 
is for advanced technical 
leads to understand. 
The infographics comprehension 
score was barely out of the average 
range at 70%, with up to 40% of 
participants indicating they 
understood little to none of what it 
means.
Rebrand Redesigns
Signal
 Concept 176
Source: Helio Survey


Users have trouble seeing 
how the information connects 
in the infographic after 
selecting a layer. 
Sentiment for the infographic is 
poor, with spikes above 10% in 
negative impressions of 
complicated, confusing, and 
overwhelming. 
Rebrand Redesigns
Signal
 Concept 176
Source: Helio Survey


Rebrand Redesigns
Decisions
Implement
Reﬁne Design
Test Iteration
Revisit Later
Do Not Pursue
Concept 176
Test Iteration
Reduce items user needs to understand on the infographic (below 13 items)
Reduce large content modules.
Test iterations of copy on page.
Source: Data Comparison Framework

schemes

Hunch Concept 178


Move forward with new dark mode 
design:
The dark mode signiﬁcantly increased all UX 
metrics tested on the Evergreen page.
Move ROI CTA down page:
Users need more clarity on what is being oﬀered 
before they want to learn about the ROI that it can 
produce.
Evergreen Redesign
Recommendation
 Concept 178
Source: Data Comparison Framework


The dark mode of the 
Evergreen page increases 
appeal towards the page 
while decreasing negative 
impressions of being stiﬀ and 
uncertain. 
UX metrics across the board 
increased for the new variation of 
the Evergreen page, especially in 
terms of sentiment and appeal.
Evergreen Redesign
Signal
 Concept 178
Source: Data Comparison Framework


Visitors aren't interested in 
ROI promises before they 
understand the platform 
oﬀering. 
See Y our ROI Impact was rated one 
of the least helpful pieces of the 
new Evergreen page despite it's 
prominent placement in the hero. 
The 'discover' CTA in the current 
hero is a more interesting CTA for 
visitors.
Evergreen Redesign
Signal
 Concept 178
Source: Data Comparison Framework


SLAs content on the current 
Evergreen page is one of the 
most helpful pieces of 
content. 
Behind the video and how it works 
content, this SLAs section was voted 
as most helpful by a signiﬁcant 
amount of participants reacting to 
the current iteration of the page.
Evergreen Redesign
Signal
 Concept 178
Source: Data Comparison Framework


Increasing infographic 
content at the top of the page 
reduces comprehension and 
sentiment. 
Both versions 2 and 3 of the dark 
mode saw a decrease in 
comprehension and a signiﬁcant 
drop in positive impressions 
towards the page.
Evergreen Redesign
Signal
 Concept 178
Source: Data Comparison Framework


Providing a color-break after 
the hero increases appeal and 
sentiment of the Evergreen 
page. 
We saw the highest sentiment score 
and lowest amount of negative 
impressions when changing the 
second modules background color 
from black to gray.
Evergreen Redesign
Signal
 Concept 178
Source: Data Comparison Framework


Evergreen Redesign
Decisions
Implement
Reﬁne Design
Test Iteration
Revisit Later
Do Not Pursue
Concept 178
Implement
Move forward with Dark Mode V4
Source: Data Comparison Framework
Test Iteration
Apply principles to light mode. SLA’s further down the page.

page.

rather than a link that can get 
lost.

doesn't attract engagement 
on the Evergreen//One page.

clicks on the page, and only 
6% of votes for the most 
helpful information on the 
page. This is low compared to 
other content like Pricing & 
Specs and the intro video that 
received up to 20% of votes 
for being most helpful.
Source: Helio Survey  
Evergreen//One Page
CONCEPT 155

RECOMMENDATION
1 Iterate and test new 
variations!
Next Steps:Provide easy link sharing from pricing page

Evergreen//One Page
CONCEPT 155
2 Provide downloadable PDF with more dense information 
3 Reduce content on page, starting with 
cost/performance tables 
4 Emphasize cost calculator oﬀer on page

pages.text:
All information copyright COMPANY NAME, LLC and ZURB, inc.
PureStorage Executive Brief
A rundown of the Helio testing activities over the last week. 
ZURB  |  August 21st, 2025

Advocacy Process
Test & Learn
Advocates use hunches to 
formulate questions for 
participants. Design concepts 
are included in those hunches.
Observations are synthesized 
from their answers. 
Signals shape the direction of 
the products and services.
Testing is done in Helio
HUNCHES QUESTIONS OBSERVATIONS SIGNALS

⚠
  Problem
We need to determine the most 
clear and engaging format for the 
contact us page. 
🧪
  UX Metrics 
Engagement, Comprehension, 
Intent
Advanced audiences of 
● IT Professionals (US)
● Tech Decision Makers (CA)
● Data Professionals (US)
● IT Managers (US)
● Tech Decision Makers (US)
Contact Us Page
CONCEPT 156
SIGNALS
●The long format of the contact page distracts users from engaging 
with the form.
●Comprehension is higher with the short format of the contact page.
●The FAQ is a more popular place for users to engage than the 
separate Partner Portal and Careers CTAs.
●The new Contact Us page outperforms Pure’s current page across all 
metrics, including comprehension, engagement, intent, and 
sentiment.
156
Source: Figma File

Designs
Source: Figma File 
Contact Us Page
CONCEPT 156

SIGNAL
The long format of the 
contact page distracts users 
from engaging with the form. 
First click engagement with 
the form was as low as 12% for 
V1, compared to over 20% for 
most other variations
Source: Data Comparison Framework  
Contact Us Page
CONCEPT 156

SIGNAL
Comprehension is higher 
with the short format of the 
contact page. 
Most of our short format 
variations have reached 
above 70% comprehension, 
compared to 68% for V1.
Source: Data Comparison Framework  
Contact Us Page
CONCEPT 156

SIGNAL
The FAQ is a more popular 
place for users to engage 
than the separate Partner 
Portal and Careers CTAs. 
Engagement with variations 
that use the FAQ in the hero 
module is higher, and 
produces more participants 
who intend to reach out 
directly to sales.
Source: Data Comparison Framework  
Contact Us Page
CONCEPT 156

SIGNAL
The FAQ is a more popular 
place for users to engage 
than the separate Partner 
Portal and Careers CTAs. 
Engagement with variations 
that use the FAQ in the hero 
module is higher, and 
produces more participants 
who intend to reach out 
directly to sales.
Source: Data Comparison Framework  
Contact Us Page
CONCEPT 156

SIGNAL
The new Contact Us page 
outperforms Pure’s current 
page across all metrics, 
including comprehension, 
engagement, intent, and 
sentiment. 
As expected, comprehension 
was almost equal across the 
two contact pages, though 
engagement with the form 
and FAQ are much higher, 
intent to use the form rather 
than the chat box is much 
higher, and sentiment (which 
was already high) slightly 
improved.
Source: Data Comparison Framework  
Contact Us Page
CONCEPT 156

RECOMMENDATION
Use short format page
1
Use FAQ in hero section of 
contact us page 
2
Source: Figma File 
Contact Us Page
CONCEPT 156
Provide form in hero on 
left-hand side
3

⚠
  Problem
Pure is setting up a Trust & 
Security Hub and need to 
compare with what competitors 
currently have out there. 
🧪
  UX Metrics 
Expectations, Sentiment, 
Helpfulness 
Advanced audiences of 
● IT Professionals (US)
● Tech Decision Makers (CA)
● Data Professionals (US)
● IT Managers (US)
● Tech Decision Makers (US)
Trust & Security Hub
CONCEPT 161
SIGNALS
● The text-ﬁrst format of the Cequence Trust Center produces the least positive 
impressions among competitors tested.
● Visitors to the Trust & Security Hub want to be educated on those subjects and 
don't need to learn more about the company.
● Hiding content under tabs like Dell's Security and Trust Center makes reduces 
the impact of content like Next Steps. 
● The concept of 'top picks' isn't clear on a trust & security hub page.
● The multiple content carousels on the Microsoft security hub makes the page feel 
likes it's trying to convey an overwhelming amount of information.
● The blog article at the top of Snowﬂake’s security page immediately deters users.
● Customer spotlights are considered by visitors to be the least 
helpful information on a trust & security page. 
Source: Figma File 
161

Designs
Source: Figma File 
Trust & Security Hub
CONCEPT 161
Dell 
Cisco
Cequence

SIGNAL
The text-ﬁrst format of the 
Cequence Trust Center 
produces the least positive 
impressions among 
competitors tested. 
The Cequence page 
produced signiﬁcant negative 
impressions of being 
confusing and overwhelming, 
and the majority of 
participants ranked their 
About Cequence module at 
the very top of the page as 
the least helpful information in 
the Trust Center.
Source: Data Comparison Framework  
Trust & Security Hub
CONCEPT 161

SIGNAL
Visitors to the Trust & 
Security Hub want to be 
educated on those subjects 
and don't need to learn more 
about the company. 
Sections like "Our Story" were 
consistently rated as the least 
helpful content on competitor 
trust and security pages.
Source: Data Comparison Framework  
Trust & Security Hub
CONCEPT 161

SIGNAL
Hiding content under tabs 
like Dell's Security and Trust 
Center makes reduces the 
impact of content like Next 
Steps. 
This was by far the least 
helpful information oﬀered on 
the Dell page since it provides 
little context as to what 
content will be provided in 
that section, compared to tabs 
like Commitment to Security 
and Compliance.
Source: Data Comparison Framework  
Trust & Security Hub
CONCEPT 161

SIGNAL
The concept of 'top picks' 
isn't clear on a trust & 
security hub page. 
This was by far the least 
helpful content on the 
Microsoft Security Hub, with 
participants saying that it feels 
promotional and would drive 
them to scroll right past that 
content.
Source: Data Comparison Framework  
Trust & Security Hub
CONCEPT 161
“I chose “Discover top picks” as least helpful 
because it’s more promotional and doesn’t 
directly teach or improve security skills it’s more 
about highlighting products than giving 
practical guidance”

SIGNAL
The multiple content 
carousels on the Microsoft 
security hub makes the page 
feel likes it's trying to convey 
an overwhelming amount of 
information. 
Sentiment for Microsoft's page 
was much lower than Dell or 
Cisco, with the primary 
negative impressions being 
overwhelming and lack of 
trust and clarity.
Source: Data Comparison Framework  
Trust & Security Hub
CONCEPT 161

Designs
Source: Figma File 
Trust & Security Hub
CONCEPT 161
AWS
Snowﬂake
NetApp

SIGNAL
The blog article at the top of 
Snowﬂake’s security page 
immediately deters users. 
This was ranked as by far the 
least helpful information on 
Snowﬂake’s page despite 
being the ﬁrst content 
presented to visitors.
Source: Data Comparison Framework  
Trust & Security Hub
CONCEPT 161

SIGNAL
Customer spotlights are 
considered by visitors to be 
the least helpful information 
on a trust & security page. 
Content like customer quotes, 
CEO quotes, and success 
stories were rated as least 
helpful across competitors like 
Snowﬂake, NetApp, and AWS.
Source: Data Comparison Framework  
Trust & Security Hub
CONCEPT 161

RECOMMENDATION
Deprioritize (or completely 
omit) information about 
the company on the Trust 
& Security page
1
Don’t hide content under tabs 
2
Source: Figma File 
Trust & Security Hub
CONCEPT 161
Provide clear value props like 
Cisco’s “Staying safe online” 
module
2

SIGNAL
Customer spotlights are 
considered by visitors to be 
the least helpful information 
on a trust & security page. 
Content like customer quotes, 
CEO quotes, and success 
stories were rated as least 
helpful across competitors like 
Snowﬂake, NetApp, and AWS.
Source: Data Comparison Framework  
Trust & Security Hub

All information copyright PureStorage, LLC and ZURB, inc.
Pure Storage Executive Brief 
A rundown of the Helio testing activities over the last week. 
ZURB  | January 31st, 2025

⚠
  Problem
The countdown is in and Pure.ai 
is being launched in days.  
Pure.AI teaser was launched to 
get the excitement up for 
everyone not just the 150 
companies that could use this.
🧪
  UX Metrics 
Engagement, Comprehension, 
Sentiment, Intent 
Advanced audiences of 
● IT Professionals (US)
● Tech Decision Makers (CA)
● Data Professionals (US)
● IT Managers (US)
● Tech Decision Makers (US)
Pure.ai Teaser
CONCEPT 142
SIGNALS
● Teasers should showcase performance with clarity. Information 
like key performance advantages, clear pricing details, and 
diﬀerentiate the product from competitors to strengthen its impact 
of teaser pages.
● Industry Norm but Room for Improvement. The teaser page 
received an average reaction (21%), as its limited information aligns 
with industry norms. 
● Clarify Diﬀerentiation. While the core concept of AI-ready data 
storage is evident, many respondents struggled to identify what 
makes this product unique.
Source: Figma File 
142

HUNCH
Source: Figma File 
Pure.ai Teaser
CONCEPT 142
Teaser 
 Form
 Launch

SIGNAL
The teaser page should 
highlight performance 
advantages ﬁrst, provide 
transparent pricing details, 
and communicate how the 
product stands out from 
competitors.
 
Source: Helio Test 
I would want to know 2 things. First, 
how much will it cost. Second, how 
will it help my company.
I’d want to know the key highlights: 
performance (speed, latency), 
capacity, and any groundbreaking 
tech it uses. I’d also be curious 
about its target use cases—whether 
it’s for enterprise, cloud, or edge 
and how it compares to existing 
solutions.
The capacities being oﬀered
How are the services accessed
The cost model of the service, and 
how the cost will change as more of 
the service is utilised.
I will be interested in learning about 
the product's features, it's potential 
impact on my daily life, any unique 
and technologies or innovation it 
employs, availability, pricing or how 
it compares to existing products.
Pure.ai Teaser
CONCEPT 142

SIGNAL
The teaser page received an 
average reaction (21%), as its 
lack of information was 
expected within the industry. 
However, some still found the 
ambiguity frustrating.
 
Source: Helio Test 
Pure.ai Teaser
CONCEPT 142
REACTION
Teaser
Very satisﬁed 26%
Somewhat satisﬁed 35%
Neutral 25%
Somewhat dissatisﬁed12%
Very dissatisﬁed 3%
21%
 Powerful is too vague, generic and 
can be claimed by anyone without 
proof. Please be more speciﬁc on the 
diﬀerentiator.
There isn't a lot of content. It's just a 
header and footer,
There is no content. Although that 
seems popular with major tech ﬁrms 
launching new products, I ﬁnd it 
annoying and typically immediately 
forget about it.

SIGNAL
While the core concept of 
AI-ready data storage comes 
through, some respondents 
either didn’t fully grasp the 
diﬀerentiators or found the 
messaging too complex. 
Comprehension was average 
(49%) with the Launch page. 
Source: Helio Test 
Pure.ai Teaser
CONCEPT 142
COMPREHENSION
Launch page 
Understood very well29%
Understood most of it46%
Understood a little 23%
Did not understand 3%
49%
“Super fast data storage using 
top-notch components to 
maximize speed, usability, and 
reliability.
Scalable, high-capacity rack 
storage for AI and HPC needs.
The product is oﬀering AI to 
manage data storage instead 
of a human carrying the 
workload.
There is too much information 
on the page with very small font 
size.

SIGNAL
While the teaser page was 
generally eﬀective in 
capturing attention, it could 
improve in clarity and 
diﬀerentiation to better serve 
its purpose.
Participants felt the teaser 
was only averagely important 
to the launch. 
Source: Helio Test 
Pure.ai Teaser
CONCEPT 142
IMPORTANCE
Teaser page 
Very Important 44%
Important 25%
Moderately important13%
Of little importance 11%
Unimportant 8%
37%
The teaser page helps to fully 
understand what this site is all 
about and the activities that 
can be done using the product.
There is nothing enticing here, 
just the normal navigation; the 
other page was more useful.
The teaser page itself doesn’t 
provide all the details, its role 
in generating initial interest 
and setting the stage for the 
product launch is deﬁnitely 
important.

Next Steps
Highlight Performance Beneﬁts Upfront
● Clearly showcase speed, scalability, and AI optimization as primary advantages.
● Use concise, bold statements or visuals to immediately convey why this product outperforms 
competitors.
1
Source: Figma File 
Pure.ai Teaser
CONCEPT 142
Improve Messaging on Diﬀerentiators
● Clarify what makes this storage solution unique beyond being “AI-ready.”
● Consider a comparison chart or bullet points summarizing key diﬀerentiators vs. competitors.
2
Teaser should Balance Engagement with Information
● If suspense is part of the teaser, ensure it builds intrigue without frustration (e.g., “Coming Soon” 
with a compelling preview, not just a vague tagline).
3

pages.text:
All information copyright Everpure and ZURB, inc.
Everpure Executive Brief (April)
A rundown of the testing activities over the last month with Helio. 
ZURB  |  April 2nd, 2026

Will users engage with the AI summaries 
across all three implementations?
UX Metrics
● Success
● Frequency
Audiences 
● Technical leader (US, UK, GR & FR)
● AI Infrastructure and Technical Leaders (Global)
● Infrastructure Owner (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Front-end & Full Stack Developers (Global)
● Non-Technical Leaders (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Financial Decision Makers (US)
Source: Figma File 
AI Summaries
Concept 182

AI summaries will be a helpful 
way to condense a large amount 
of support information
Assumption
● “Book” summary may not be intuitive
● Location of publication summary is hard 
to ﬁnd
AI Summaries
Hunch Concept 182
Source: Figma File

AI Summaries
Design Concept 182
Source: Figma File 
V1 - Results V2 - Book V3 - Publication

Visually diﬀerentiate between 
results summaries and book 
summaries CTAs 
Participants were unable to 
successfully choose between a 
full results summary and an 
individual book summary when 
both are present on the page.
AI Summaries
Recommendation Concept 182
Source: Data Comparison Sheet

Users ﬁnd equal value in all of 
the AI summary 
implementations and would 
use them each frequently. 
The frequency score was almost 
equal for each of the three types of AI 
summaries at around 82%. Users 
indicated they like the idea of a 
summarized snippet of information so 
that they don't have to comb through 
the search results themselves.
AI Summaries
Signal Concept 182
Source: Data Comparison Sheet 
“having an AI summary of a full help 
article allows me to quickly 
understand the key purpose, 
requirements, and steps without 
reading through the entire 
document.”
“highlights important details, helping 
me grasp the overall message faster. 
It's useful.”
“[Good for] when the search results is 
too long and complex.”
“AI is not a bad tool to use at all. 
There's no loss trying to use it, and it 
really helps you get things done 
quicker”
FREQUENCY
Book Summary 81%
FREQUENCY
Results Summary 82%
FREQUENCY
Publication Summary 83%

The diﬀerence between a 
summary of search results and 
individual books is unclear. 
Finding the AI book summary feature 
showed the lowest success among 
the three implementations tested, 
despite decent success when looking 
for the full search results summary on 
the same screen.
AI Summaries
Signal Concept 182
Source: Data Comparison Sheet

AI Summaries
Decisions Concept 182
Implement
Reﬁne Design
Test Iteration
Revisit Later
Do Not Pursue
Test Iteration
Based on low usability success of book summaries, we’ll need to update the visual presentation of the CTA 
to allow users to interact with this valuable tool.
Quelyn to present validation of value to stakeholders and considerations for differentiating the Summarize 
Book CTAs.
Source: Data Comparison Sheet

Where can we identify improvements to 
the UX across key pages in the 
Knowledge Portal?
UX Metrics
● Expectations
● Satisfaction
● Sentiment
● Intent
● Success
● Eﬀort
Audiences 
● Technical leader (US, UK, GR & FR)
● AI Infrastructure and Technical Leaders (Global)
● Infrastructure Owner (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Front-end & Full Stack Developers (Global)
● Non-Technical Leaders (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Financial Decision Makers (US)
Source: Figma File 
UX Feedback
Concept 184

We can identify user experience 
issues that need to be addressed 
acrossed key Knowledge Portal pages
Assumption
● Homepage and Landing Page may feel 
overwhelming now
● Content under header menus is not 
immediately intuitive
● Search Page ﬁlters are clear
UX Feedback
Hunch Concept 184
Source: Figma File

UX Feedback
Design Concept 184
Source: Figma File 
Home Product Page - Landing Reader Page

UX Feedback
Design Concept 184
Source: Figma File 
Search Headers

Homepage: 
Add Browse by Category label to the products grid, as we 
know participants were eager to engage with this content 
in the baseline.
Landing Page: 
Reduce text in individual content cards to make page more 
scannable.
Search Page: 
Group ﬁlters so that users can more quickly ﬁnd ﬁlters by 
category.
Headers: 
Conduct card sort/tree testing to determine where users 
naturally expect to group these pages.
Reader page: 
Clarify action icons in the top right corner to ensure users 
can engage with most important features.
UX Feedback
Recommendation Concept 184
Source: Data Comparison Sheet

The new Everpure Knowledge 
Portal homepage drives intent 
to engage with the search bar 
rather than choosing a product 
or technical release below. 
Intent is higher for the new version of 
the homepage due to participants 
gravitating towards the search bar. 
That means there is less intent to 
search using other elements on the 
page.
UX Feedback - Homepage
Signal Concept 184
Source: Data Comparison Sheet

Users appreciate the Browse 
by Category label for the 
product support information. 
Up to 33% of users indicated they 
would use Browse by Category in the 
baseline, compared to just 7% of 
participants who would browse by 
product in the new version, despite 
the fact that they show the same 
categories. This indicates the 
positioning of the information is 
important on the support homepage.
UX Feedback - Homepage
Signal Concept 184
Source: Data Comparison Sheet

The jump-to menu saves up to 
15 seconds for the half of 
participants who take action 
there. 
The median time to action for the 
jump-to menu was 20 seconds, 
compared to 35 seconds for 
participants who scrolled down to the 
speciﬁc category and article.
UX Feedback - Landing Page
Signal Concept 184
Source: Helio Test

The new knowledge portal 
landing page feels 
overwhelming for users looking 
for speciﬁc information. 
The landing page produced spikes in 
several negative impressions above 
10%, such as overwhelming (28%), 
complicated (16%), and diﬃcult (11%). 
Participants explained that there is 
too much information to sort through 
on the page.
UX Feedback - Landing Page
Signal Concept 184
Source: Helio Test 
“I love the eﬃcient way that every 
option is laid out in order, but there is 
a lot to take in; but I guess this is 
unavoidable.”
“There is a lot going on with this 
page; you should have buttons that 
launch diﬀerent parts of the website 
with the corresponding options on 
the respective pages.”
“it's also overwhelming because 
there are just too many categories, 
so maybe this format is not the right 
one for this kind of page.”
“there's too much to look at or 
everything is spread out so much it 
would be easier to look at a list”
Landing Page

Users expect to save a search 
criteria under the Saved 
Searches menu. 
Usability of the search page features 
was reduced by the 30% of 
participants who didn't notice the 
save feature in the top right of the 
article and instead tried to take that 
action under the Saved Searches tab.
UX Feedback - Search Page
Signal Concept 184
Source: Helio Test

Users don't feel the search 
page ﬁlters provide quick 
access to the content they 
need. 
Sentiment of the search page was 
reduced to average levels due to 
negative impressions of being 
overwhelming and low positive 
impressions of the ﬁlters feeling quick 
to use.
UX Feedback - Search Page
Signal Concept 184
Source: Helio Test

Users are forced to guess what 
content live under the 
navigation headers. 
Usability was poor for each of the 
three dropdown menus in the 
navigation. Each action showed less 
than 50% success, much lower than 
the expected 65%.
UX Feedback - Headers
Signal Concept 184
Source: Helio Test

Users gravitate towards the 
search bar when they can't ﬁnd 
the items they need in the top 
navigation. 
Many participants, up to 63% of 
participants clicked on the search bar 
to ﬁnd speciﬁc information held under 
one of the three top nav dropdown 
menus.
UX Feedback - Headers
Signal Concept 184
Source: Helio Test

Users feel the reader page is 
overwhelming due to the 
length of the content and 
numerous images. 
Despite the helpfulness of images 
highlighting where to take action in 
the product, the length of the reader 
page produces signiﬁcant negative 
impressions of being overwhelming 
and complicated.
UX Feedback - Reader Page
Signal Concept 184
Source: Helio Test

Usability of the reader page is 
hindered by unclear action 
icons in the top right corner. 
Only about 50% of participants were 
successful ﬁnding actions such as 
share and save for later using the icon 
actions. Many explained that the icons 
aren't immediately intuitive.
UX Feedback - Reader Page
Signal Concept 184
Source: Helio Test 
“I would not say it is hard, but I 
would have to hover the icons to 
see which one is what I looking 
for/trying to do.”
“Not simple to locate actions.”
“Some of the buttons were not 
familiar to me to know which one 
was the correct one to press. I 
think if they put the button names 
underneath they would be more 
eﬀective...”
“It would be useful if the icons on 
the top right had the ability to 
'hover over' with an explanation 
pop-up saying what the options 
function is”

UX Feedback
Decisions Concept 184
Implement
Reﬁne Design
Test Iteration
Revisit Later
Do Not Pursue
Reﬁne Design
Quelyn to take action refining these page designs in the following ways:
Homepage - add Browse by Category label
Landing Page - reduce text for increase scannability
Search Page - group filters under accordions
Headers - explore alternate labels for header menus
Reader page - provide hover tooltips for icons and use buttons for important actions
Source: Data Comparison Sheet

Can we improve engagement and 
impressions towards the event page 
through more consistent visuals?
UX Metrics
● Engagement
● Success
● Sentiment
Audiences 
● Technical leader (US, UK, GR & FR)
● AI Infrastructure and Technical Leaders 
(Global)
● Infrastructure Owner (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Front-end & Full Stack Developers (Global)
● Non-Technical Leaders (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Financial Decision Makers (US)
Source: Figma File 
Event Pages
Concept 151

The current Events page lacks in 
consistency and clarity of navigating 
Everpure events.
Assumption
● Events thumbnails are inconsistent and 
reduce impressions of the page
● Ambiguous event labels reduce usability of 
accessing certain content on the page
Event Pages
Hunch Concept 151
Source: Figma File

Event Pages
Design Concept 151
Source: Figma File 
baseline v2v1

Reduce competing visual 
styles and shapes on the page 
Signiﬁcant negative impressions 
stem from the overwhelming 
blend of diﬀerent visual elements 
on the Events page.
Event Pages
Recommendation Concept 151
Source: Helio Test

The trade show module does a 
good job of attracting attention 
at the top of the events page. 
Almost an equal amount of 
participants (22%) interacted with the 
trade shows on ﬁrst click as the 
search bar (24%).
Event Pages
Signal Concept 151
First click
Source: Helio Test

Competing shapes and 
elements on the events page 
result in an overwhelming 
layout. 
Negative impressions of being 
confusing and overwhelming spiked 
well above the expected 10% limit, 
with participants explaining that the 
visuals on the page are busy and 
inconsistent.
Event Pages
Signal Concept 151
“everything is everywhere making it 
kinda diﬃcult to pinpoint what i am 
looking for easily.”
“There are a lot of elements and the 
fact that everything is diﬀerent in size 
makes it confusing.”
“The page seems too busy - I have a 
hard time reading the text - it's too 
small, there's too much of it, and I 
don't know what I'm looking at 
beyond blocks of color.”
“There's a lot going on on this page. 
There are too many things to look at 
all at once. I would prefer a simpliﬁed 
and more streamlined page with 
more broad categories that I could 
click through to get to other 
information.”
Events Page
Source: Helio Test

Users ﬁnd it hard to locate 
speciﬁc topics and events 
on the current page. 
Poor sentiment and average 
usability eﬀort scores were the 
result of an overwhelming 
amount of diﬀerent elements on 
the page.
Event Pages
Signal Concept 151
“I can see featured events, but I 
dont know if the event i want is 
featured, yet I have to scroll a 
long way down to get to all 
events.”
“Because of the amount of 
elements it makes it hard to ﬁnd 
all elements.”
“I ﬁnd this to be a hard on the 
eyes design and not easy to 
navigate.”
“Again, there is so much to look at. 
Simplify it to make it more user 
friendly.”
Events Page
Source: Helio Test

Event Pages
Decisions Concept 151
Implement
Reﬁne Design
Test Iteration
Revisit Later
Do Not Pursue
Test Iteration
Based on negative impressions with the current Events page, we need to test our updated variations to see 
if more consistent thumbnail images make users feel better about the page.
We should also pursue a variation that reduces the competing shapes on the page to simplify the 
experience for users trying to find specific events.
Source: Helio Test

full_text:
All information copyright Everpure and ZURB, inc.
Everpure Executive Brief (April)
A rundown of the testing activities over the last month with Helio. 
ZURB  |  April 2nd, 2026


Will users engage with the AI summaries 
across all three implementations?
UX Metrics
● Success
● Frequency
Audiences 
● Technical leader (US, UK, GR & FR)
● AI Infrastructure and Technical Leaders (Global)
● Infrastructure Owner (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Front-end & Full Stack Developers (Global)
● Non-Technical Leaders (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Financial Decision Makers (US)
Source: Figma File 
AI Summaries
Concept 182


AI summaries will be a helpful 
way to condense a large amount 
of support information
Assumption
● “Book” summary may not be intuitive
● Location of publication summary is hard 
to ﬁnd
AI Summaries
Hunch Concept 182
Source: Figma File 


AI Summaries
Design Concept 182
Source: Figma File 
V1 - Results V2 - Book V3 - Publication


Visually diﬀerentiate between 
results summaries and book 
summaries CTAs 
Participants were unable to 
successfully choose between a 
full results summary and an 
individual book summary when 
both are present on the page.
AI Summaries
Recommendation Concept 182
Source: Data Comparison Sheet 


Users ﬁnd equal value in all of 
the AI summary 
implementations and would 
use them each frequently. 
The frequency score was almost 
equal for each of the three types of AI 
summaries at around 82%. Users 
indicated they like the idea of a 
summarized snippet of information so 
that they don't have to comb through 
the search results themselves.
AI Summaries
Signal Concept 182
Source: Data Comparison Sheet 
“having an AI summary of a full help 
article allows me to quickly 
understand the key purpose, 
requirements, and steps without 
reading through the entire 
document.”
“highlights important details, helping 
me grasp the overall message faster. 
It's useful.”
“[Good for] when the search results is 
too long and complex.”
“AI is not a bad tool to use at all. 
There's no loss trying to use it, and it 
really helps you get things done 
quicker”
FREQUENCY
Book Summary 81%
FREQUENCY
Results Summary 82%
FREQUENCY
Publication Summary 83%

The diﬀerence between a 
summary of search results and 
individual books is unclear. 
Finding the AI book summary feature 
showed the lowest success among 
the three implementations tested, 
despite decent success when looking 
for the full search results summary on 
the same screen.
AI Summaries
Signal Concept 182
Source: Data Comparison Sheet 


AI Summaries
Decisions Concept 182
Implement
Reﬁne Design
Test Iteration
Revisit Later
Do Not Pursue
Test Iteration
Based on low usability success of book summaries, we’ll need to update the visual presentation of the CTA 
to allow users to interact with this valuable tool.
Quelyn to present validation of value to stakeholders and considerations for differentiating the Summarize 
Book CTAs.
Source: Data Comparison Sheet 

Where can we identify improvements to 
the UX across key pages in the 
Knowledge Portal?
UX Metrics
● Expectations
● Satisfaction
● Sentiment
● Intent
● Success
● Eﬀort
Audiences 
● Technical leader (US, UK, GR & FR)
● AI Infrastructure and Technical Leaders (Global)
● Infrastructure Owner (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Front-end & Full Stack Developers (Global)
● Non-Technical Leaders (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Financial Decision Makers (US)
Source: Figma File 
UX Feedback
Concept 184


We can identify user experience 
issues that need to be addressed 
acrossed key Knowledge Portal pages
Assumption
● Homepage and Landing Page may feel 
overwhelming now
● Content under header menus is not 
immediately intuitive
● Search Page ﬁlters are clear
UX Feedback
Hunch Concept 184
Source: Figma File 


UX Feedback
Design Concept 184
Source: Figma File 
Home Product Page - Landing Reader Page


UX Feedback
Design Concept 184
Source: Figma File 
Search Headers


Homepage: 
Add Browse by Category label to the products grid, as we 
know participants were eager to engage with this content 
in the baseline.
Landing Page: 
Reduce text in individual content cards to make page more 
scannable.
Search Page: 
Group ﬁlters so that users can more quickly ﬁnd ﬁlters by 
category.
Headers: 
Conduct card sort/tree testing to determine where users 
naturally expect to group these pages.
Reader page: 
Clarify action icons in the top right corner to ensure users 
can engage with most important features.
UX Feedback
Recommendation Concept 184
Source: Data Comparison Sheet 


The new Everpure Knowledge 
Portal homepage drives intent 
to engage with the search bar 
rather than choosing a product 
or technical release below. 
Intent is higher for the new version of 
the homepage due to participants 
gravitating towards the search bar. 
That means there is less intent to 
search using other elements on the 
page.
UX Feedback - Homepage
Signal Concept 184
Source: Data Comparison Sheet 


Users appreciate the Browse 
by Category label for the 
product support information. 
Up to 33% of users indicated they 
would use Browse by Category in the 
baseline, compared to just 7% of 
participants who would browse by 
product in the new version, despite 
the fact that they show the same 
categories. This indicates the 
positioning of the information is 
important on the support homepage.
UX Feedback - Homepage
Signal Concept 184
Source: Data Comparison Sheet 

The jump-to menu saves up to 
15 seconds for the half of 
participants who take action 
there. 
The median time to action for the 
jump-to menu was 20 seconds, 
compared to 35 seconds for 
participants who scrolled down to the 
speciﬁc category and article.
UX Feedback - Landing Page
Signal Concept 184
Source: Helio Test 


The new knowledge portal 
landing page feels 
overwhelming for users looking 
for speciﬁc information. 
The landing page produced spikes in 
several negative impressions above 
10%, such as overwhelming (28%), 
complicated (16%), and diﬃcult (11%). 
Participants explained that there is 
too much information to sort through 
on the page.
UX Feedback - Landing Page
Signal Concept 184
Source: Helio Test 
“I love the eﬃcient way that every 
option is laid out in order, but there is 
a lot to take in; but I guess this is 
unavoidable.”
“There is a lot going on with this 
page; you should have buttons that 
launch diﬀerent parts of the website 
with the corresponding options on 
the respective pages.”
“it's also overwhelming because 
there are just too many categories, 
so maybe this format is not the right 
one for this kind of page.”
“there's too much to look at or 
everything is spread out so much it 
would be easier to look at a list”
Landing Page

Users expect to save a search 
criteria under the Saved 
Searches menu. 
Usability of the search page features 
was reduced by the 30% of 
participants who didn't notice the 
save feature in the top right of the 
article and instead tried to take that 
action under the Saved Searches tab.
UX Feedback - Search Page
Signal Concept 184
Source: Helio Test 


Users don't feel the search 
page ﬁlters provide quick 
access to the content they 
need. 
Sentiment of the search page was 
reduced to average levels due to 
negative impressions of being 
overwhelming and low positive 
impressions of the ﬁlters feeling quick 
to use.
UX Feedback - Search Page
Signal Concept 184
Source: Helio Test 


Users are forced to guess what 
content live under the 
navigation headers. 
Usability was poor for each of the 
three dropdown menus in the 
navigation. Each action showed less 
than 50% success, much lower than 
the expected 65%.
UX Feedback - Headers
Signal Concept 184
Source: Helio Test 


Users gravitate towards the 
search bar when they can't ﬁnd 
the items they need in the top 
navigation. 
Many participants, up to 63% of 
participants clicked on the search bar 
to ﬁnd speciﬁc information held under 
one of the three top nav dropdown 
menus.
UX Feedback - Headers
Signal Concept 184
Source: Helio Test 


Users feel the reader page is 
overwhelming due to the 
length of the content and 
numerous images. 
Despite the helpfulness of images 
highlighting where to take action in 
the product, the length of the reader 
page produces signiﬁcant negative 
impressions of being overwhelming 
and complicated.
UX Feedback - Reader Page
Signal Concept 184
Source: Helio Test 


Usability of the reader page is 
hindered by unclear action 
icons in the top right corner. 
Only about 50% of participants were 
successful ﬁnding actions such as 
share and save for later using the icon 
actions. Many explained that the icons 
aren't immediately intuitive.
UX Feedback - Reader Page
Signal Concept 184
Source: Helio Test 
“I would not say it is hard, but I 
would have to hover the icons to 
see which one is what I looking 
for/trying to do.”
“Not simple to locate actions.”
“Some of the buttons were not 
familiar to me to know which one 
was the correct one to press. I 
think if they put the button names 
underneath they would be more 
eﬀective...”
“It would be useful if the icons on 
the top right had the ability to 
'hover over' with an explanation 
pop-up saying what the options 
function is”

UX Feedback
Decisions Concept 184
Implement
Reﬁne Design
Test Iteration
Revisit Later
Do Not Pursue
Reﬁne Design
Quelyn to take action refining these page designs in the following ways:
Homepage - add Browse by Category label
Landing Page - reduce text for increase scannability
Search Page - group filters under accordions
Headers - explore alternate labels for header menus
Reader page - provide hover tooltips for icons and use buttons for important actions
Source: Data Comparison Sheet 


Can we improve engagement and 
impressions towards the event page 
through more consistent visuals?
UX Metrics
● Engagement
● Success
● Sentiment
Audiences 
● Technical leader (US, UK, GR & FR)
● AI Infrastructure and Technical Leaders 
(Global)
● Infrastructure Owner (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Front-end & Full Stack Developers (Global)
● Non-Technical Leaders (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Financial Decision Makers (US)
Source: Figma File 
Event Pages
Concept 151


The current Events page lacks in 
consistency and clarity of navigating 
Everpure events.
Assumption
● Events thumbnails are inconsistent and 
reduce impressions of the page
● Ambiguous event labels reduce usability of 
accessing certain content on the page
Event Pages
Hunch Concept 151
Source: Figma File 


Event Pages
Design Concept 151
Source: Figma File 
baseline v2v1


Reduce competing visual 
styles and shapes on the page 
Signiﬁcant negative impressions 
stem from the overwhelming 
blend of diﬀerent visual elements 
on the Events page.
Event Pages
Recommendation Concept 151
Source: Helio Test 


The trade show module does a 
good job of attracting attention 
at the top of the events page. 
Almost an equal amount of 
participants (22%) interacted with the 
trade shows on ﬁrst click as the 
search bar (24%).
Event Pages
Signal Concept 151
First click
Source: Helio Test 

Competing shapes and 
elements on the events page 
result in an overwhelming 
layout. 
Negative impressions of being 
confusing and overwhelming spiked 
well above the expected 10% limit, 
with participants explaining that the 
visuals on the page are busy and 
inconsistent.
Event Pages
Signal Concept 151
“everything is everywhere making it 
kinda diﬃcult to pinpoint what i am 
looking for easily.”
“There are a lot of elements and the 
fact that everything is diﬀerent in size 
makes it confusing.”
“The page seems too busy - I have a 
hard time reading the text - it's too 
small, there's too much of it, and I 
don't know what I'm looking at 
beyond blocks of color.”
“There's a lot going on on this page. 
There are too many things to look at 
all at once. I would prefer a simpliﬁed 
and more streamlined page with 
more broad categories that I could 
click through to get to other 
information.”
Events Page
Source: Helio Test 

Users ﬁnd it hard to locate 
speciﬁc topics and events 
on the current page. 
Poor sentiment and average 
usability eﬀort scores were the 
result of an overwhelming 
amount of diﬀerent elements on 
the page.
Event Pages
Signal Concept 151
“I can see featured events, but I 
dont know if the event i want is 
featured, yet I have to scroll a 
long way down to get to all 
events.”
“Because of the amount of 
elements it makes it hard to ﬁnd 
all elements.”
“I ﬁnd this to be a hard on the 
eyes design and not easy to 
navigate.”
“Again, there is so much to look at. 
Simplify it to make it more user 
friendly.”
Events Page
Source: Helio Test 

Event Pages
Decisions Concept 151
Implement
Reﬁne Design
Test Iteration
Revisit Later
Do Not Pursue
Test Iteration
Based on negative impressions with the current Events page, we need to test our updated variations to see 
if more consistent thumbnail images make users feel better about the page.
We should also pursue a variation that reduces the competing shapes on the page to simplify the 
experience for users trying to find specific events.
Source: Helio Test

Pure Storage Executive Brief
A rundown of the Helio testing activities over the last week. 
ZURB  |  November 6th, 2025

We have three early versions of a 
navigation wireframe to compare.
UX Metrics
● Helpfulness
● Eﬀort
● Preference  
Audiences 
● Technical leader (US, UK, GR & FR)
● AI Infrastructure and Technical Leaders (Global)
● Infrastructure Owner (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Front-end & Full Stack Developers (Global)
● Non-Technical Leaders (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Financial Decision Makers (US)
Early Nav Explorations
Concept 169

Early ﬁndings from wireframes will help us 
build towards a nav menu that works.
Assumption
● Providing imagery in nav menu will increase 
engagement with key products
● Using a full width bar at the bottom of the nav will 
provide a place to emphasize key oﬀerings
Early Nav Explorations 
Hunch Concept 169

Early Nav Explorations 
Design
 Concept 169
V2V1
Source: Helio Test

Pure Storage Executive Brief
A rundown of the Helio testing activities over the last week. 
ZURB  |  September 11th, 2025

The team used AI to set up two versions 
of a new platform conﬁgurator 
assessment and need to determine the 
best direction for the new tool.
UX Metrics
● Expectations
● Importance
● Comprehension  
Audiences 
● Technical leader (US, UK, GR & FR)
● AI Infrastructure and Technical Leaders (Global)
● Infrastructure Owner (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Front-end & Full Stack Developers (Global)
● Non-Technical Leaders (US, UK, GR & FR)
● Financial Decision Makers (US)
Platform Conﬁgurator
Concept 162

The platform conﬁgurator prototypes are a 
great start but may not present the right 
questions to users in the assessment.
Assumption
● Business priorities should be moved up in the ﬂow
● Persona isn’t the ideal starting place on the ﬁrst 
screen of the assessment
● Workloads page may be too technical for a 
non-technical audience
Platform Conﬁgurator 
Hunch Concept 162

Platform Conﬁgurator 
Design
 Concept 162
V2V1

Move business priorities up in the ﬂow:
These were ranked as the #1 and #2 most 
important information to provide in both versions of 
the conﬁgurator.
Lead with industry rather than role:
Industry seems to provide more clear picture of the 
business’s data needs rather than the context of 
the individual user’s role.
Maintain workloads and move up in ﬂow:
This was deemed as a very important piece of 
information to provide by technical participants.
V1
Platform Conﬁgurator
Recommendation
 Concept 162
Source: Helio Test #1 and Test #2

Role is much more important 
for non-technical leaders to 
convey. 
Despite being ranked at or 
near the bottom of the list for 
the overall audience, when 
ﬁltering by just non-tech 
leaders and ﬁnancial decision 
makers, role rises signiﬁcantly 
towards the top of the ranking.
Platform Conﬁgurator
Signal
Source: Helio Test #1 and Test #2 
Concept 162
Full 
Audience
Non-Tech & 
Finance

Workloads and business 
priorities feel like they have 
the greatest impact on 
conﬁguration. 
Both were ranked highly 
across both iterations of our 
platform conﬁgurator starting 
page, with business priorities 
speciﬁcally staying in the top 2 
for both rankings.
Platform Conﬁgurator
Signal
Source: Helio Test #1 and Test #2 
Concept 162

Industry is deemed more 
important than personal role 
in the platform conﬁgurator. 
Participants explained that 
industry can give a great 
starting place for the type of 
workloads and web 
environments they deal with, 
while personal role is more 
contextual for the company 
rather than being key for 
conﬁguration.
Platform Conﬁgurator
Signal
Source: Helio Test #1 and Test #2 
Concept 162
“Role is important so they can focus the 
information to their speciﬁc audience, but is less 
important than the other areas.”
“[roles] matter but they are more about how to 
implement and present the solution”
“I wanted to see the basics behind our needs 
talked about before asking basically what my title 
was.”
“my own role is not as important as the previous 
ones. It doesn't take priority over the others in 
having to get my business data storage needs”

Platform Conﬁgurator
Decisions
Decision 1:  Move Business Priorities up in ﬂow
Decision 2:  Lead with industry rather than role on persona page
Decision 3:  Move Workloads up in ﬂow
Decision 4:  Emphasize industries more
Implement
Reﬁne Design
Test Iteration
Revisit Later

CPQ Sales Tool 
Hunch Concept 164

CPQ Sales Tool 
Design
 Concept 164
2 - Conﬁgure1 - Catalog
 4 - Quote3 - Services

CPQ Sales Tool: Full Flow
Recommendation
Source: Helio AI Assessment 
Concept 164
1. Enhance user conﬁdence by providing visual 
conﬁrmation messages after actions are completed.
2. Simplify the conﬁguration screens by collapsing 
advanced options and oﬀering tooltips to help new 
users.
3. Incorporate visual summaries or dashboards to give 
quick overviews of conﬁgurations, improving user 
decision-making.

CPQ Sales Tool: Catalog
Recommendation
Source: Helio AI Assessment 
Concept 164
1. Enhance product card clarity by simplifying text and 
using bullet points for key features, making it easier 
for users to scan information.
2. Streamline ﬁlter options and improve visual 
hierarchy to prevent overwhelm and improve user 
navigation.
3. Integrate tooltips and visual aids to assist new users 
in quickly understanding product features and 
conﬁgurations.

CPQ Sales Tool: Conﬁgure
Recommendation
Source: Helio AI Assessment 
Concept 164
1. Streamline the interface by consolidating similar 
product options and using collapsible sections to 
enhance usability as oﬀerings grow.
2. Add visual elements such as charts and increase the 
visibility of tooltips to engage users and support 
their understanding of conﬁgurations.
3. Implement real-time validation and error feedback 
to build user conﬁdence and reduce errors during 
the quoting process.

CPQ Sales Tool: Services
Recommendation
Source: Helio AI Assessment 
Concept 164
1. Integrate visual charts to aid users in understanding 
complex conﬁgurations and enhance 
decision-making speed.
2. Improve the visibility of primary actions, like the 
'Next' button, to assist in guiding users through the 
process more eﬃciently.
3. Implement immediate feedback mechanisms for 
selections to boost user conﬁdence and ensure a 
more intuitive experience.

CPQ Sales Tool: Quote

 4 - Quote3 - Services

CPQ Sales Tool: Full Flow
Recommendation
Source: Helio AI Assessment 
Concept 164
1. Enhance user conﬁdence by providing visual 
conﬁrmation messages after actions are completed.
2. Simplify the conﬁguration screens by collapsing 
advanced options and oﬀering tooltips to help new 
users.
3. Incorporate visual summaries or dashboards to give 
quick overviews of conﬁgurations, improving user 
decision-making.


CPQ Sales Tool: Catalog
Recommendation
Source: Helio AI Assessment 
Concept 164
1. Enhance product card clarity by simplifying text and 
using bullet points for key features, making it easier 
for users to scan information.
2. Streamline ﬁlter options and improve visual 
hierarchy to prevent overwhelm and improve user 
navigation.
3. Integrate tooltips and visual aids to assist new users 
in quickly understanding product features and 
conﬁgurations.


CPQ Sales Tool: Conﬁgure
Recommendation
Source: Helio AI Assessment 
Concept 164
1. Streamline the interface by consolidating similar 
product options and using collapsible sections to 
enhance usability as oﬀerings grow.
2. Add visual elements such as charts and increase the 
visibility of tooltips to engage users and support 
their understanding of conﬁgurations.
3. Implement real-time validation and error feedback 
to build user conﬁdence and reduce errors during 
the quoting process.


CPQ Sales Tool: Services
Recommendation
Source: Helio AI Assessment 
Concept 164
1. Integrate visual charts to aid users in understanding 
complex conﬁgurations and enhance 
decision-making speed.
2. Improve the visibility of primary actions, like the 
'Next' button, to assist in guiding users through the 
process more eﬃciently.
3. Implement immediate feedback mechanisms for 
selections to boost user conﬁdence and ensure a 
more intuitive experience.


CPQ Sales Tool: Quote
Recommendation
Source: Helio AI Assessment 
Concept 164
1. Incorporate tooltips for icons to improve user 
understanding and reduce the need for trial 
and error.
2. Streamline data presentation and navigation to 
enhance clarity and reduce cognitive load for 
users.
3. Add visual aids like charts and highlighted 
steps to support quick comprehension and 
decision-making.
